titleStmt.title: Systematic construction of topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum gates based on many-body Majorana fermion interactions

analytic.title: Systematic construction of topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum gates based on many-body Majorana fermion interactions

application.desc: GROBID - A machine learning software for extracting information from scholarly documents

div.p: Topological quantum computation by way of braiding of Majorana fermions is not universal quantum computation. There are several attempts to make universal quantum computation by introducing some additional quantum gates or quantum states. However, there is an embedding problem that M -qubit gates cannot be embedded straightforwardly in N qubits for N > M . This problem is inherent to the Majorana system, where logical qubits are different from physical qubits because braiding operations preserve the fermion parity. By introducing 2N -body interactions of Majorana fermions, topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum computation is shown to be possible. Especially, we make a systematic construction of the C n Z gate, C n NOT gate and the C n SWAP gate.

A quantum computer is a promising next generation computer [1][2][3]. In order to execute any quantum algorithms, universal quantum computation is necessary [4][5][6]. There are various approaches to realize universal computation including superconductors [7], photonic systems [8], quantum dots [9], trapped ions [10] and nuclear magnetic resonance [11,12]. The Solovay-Kitaev theorem dictates that only the Hadamard gate, the π/4 phase-shift gate and the CNOT gate are enough for universal quantum computation. These one and two-qubit quantum gates can be embedded to larger qubits straightforwardly in these approaches.

Braiding of Majorana fermions is the most promising method for topological quantum computation [13][14][15][16][17]. There are various approaches to materialize Majorana fermions such as fractional quantum Hall effects [16,[18][19][20], topological superconductors [21][22][23][24][25][26][27] and Kitaev spin liquids [28,29]. However, it can generate only a part of Clifford gate [30,31]. The entire Clifford gates are generated for two qubits but not for more than three qubits [31]. Furthermore, only the Clifford gates are not enough to exceed classical computers, which is known as the Gottesman-Knill theorem [32][33][34].

There are several attempts to make universal quantum computation based on Majorana fermions [20,24,30,[35][36][37][38][39][40][41]. In the Majorana system, it is necessary to construct logical qubits from physical qubits by taking a parity definite basis, because braiding preserves the fermion parity. It makes logical qubits nonlocal. It is a nontrivial problem to embed a nonlocal Mqubit quantum gate in the N -qubit system with N > M . Hence, even if the Hadamard gate, the π/4 phase-shift gate and the CNOT gate are constructed, it is not enough for universal quantum computation in the N -qubit system unless this embedding problem is resolved.

In this paper, we systematically construct various quantum gates for universal quantum computation by introducing 2N -body interactions of Majorana fermions preserving the fermion parity. We have required the fermion parity preservation because it is beneficial to use the standard braiding process as much as possible due to its topological protection. By combining topological quantum gates generated by braiding and additional quantum gates generated by many-body interactions of Majorana fermions, topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum computation is possible. It would be more robust than conventional universal quantum computation because the quantum gates generated by braiding are topologically protected. We systematically construct arbitrary C n -phase shift gates, the Hadamard gate, C n NOT gates and C n SWAP gates in the N -qubit system by this generalization.
Supplementary Materials are prepared for detailed analysis in the case of small qubits to make clear a general analysis for the N -qubit system.
Physical qubits and logical qubits: Majorana fermions are described by operators γ α satisfying the anticommutation relations {γ α , γ β } = 2δ αβ .
The braid operator is defined by [14]
div.formula: B αβ = exp π 4 γ β γ α = 1 √ 2 (1 + γ β γ α ) .(2)
div.p: It satisfies B 4 αβ = 1 and there is a corresponding anti-braiding operator B -1 αβ = B 3 αβ . The qubit basis is defined by [14] |n
div.formula: N +1 n N • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ physical ≡ c † 1 n1 c † 2 n2 • • • c † N n N c † N +1 n N +1 |0⟩ ,(3)
div.p: with n α = 0 or 1, where ordinary fermion operators are constructed from two Majorana fermions as
2N + 4 Majorana fermions constitute N + 1 physical qubits. The braiding operation preserves the fermion parity P αβ ≡ iγ β γ α , where it commutes with the braid operator B αβ ,
div.formula: [B αβ , P αβ ] = 0.(5)
div.p: It means that if we start with the even parity state |00 • • • 0⟩ physical , the states after any braiding process should have even fermion parity. Therefore, in order to construct [42][43][44]. There are N ! correspondences between the logical and physical qubits in general. However, we adopt the following unique correspondence. When the logical qubit
div.formula: N logical qubits |n N • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ logical , N + 1 physical qubits n ′ N +1 • • • n ′ 2 n ′ 1 physical are necessary
|n N • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ logical is given, we associate to it a physical qubit |n N • • • n 2 n 1 n 0 ⟩ physical by
div.p: adding one qubit n 0 uniquely so that N α=0 n α = 0 mod 2. Alternatively, when a physical qubit |n N • • • n 2 n 1 n 0 ⟩ logical is given, we associate to it a logical qubit |n N • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ physical just by eliminating the qubit n 0 . An example reads as follows,

This correspondence is different from those in the previous works [13,[43][44][45][46][47]. Accordingly, the detailed braiding process for quantum gates are slightly different from the previous ones [43][44][45][46][47].

2N -body interactions: A generic operator involving two Majorana fermions γ α and γ β is expressed as U αβ = a 1 + a 2 γ α + a 3 γ β + a 4 γ β γ α , since higher-order terms γ p α γ q β are absent for p ≥ 2 and q ≥ 2 because of the relations
div.formula: γ 2 α = γ 2 β = 1.
div.p: Then, by imposing the parity conservation condition [U αβ , P αβ ] = 0 with the fermion-parity operator
div.formula: P αβ = γ β γα, it is restricted to U αβ = a 1 + a 4 γ β γ α . Further- more, the unitary condition U αβ U αβ = U † αβ U αβ = 1 leads to the representation of U αβ in the form of U αβ (θ) = exp(θγ β γ α ) = cos θ + γ β γ α sin θ,(7)
(U αβ (θ)) -1 = exp(-θγ β γ α ) = cos θ -γ β γ α sin θ.(8)
div.p: The choice θ = π/4 corresponds to the braiding operation. In general, θ can take an arbitrary value. This operator transforms the Majorana operators as
div.formula: U αβ (θ) γ α (U αβ (θ)) -1 = γ α cos 2θ + γ β sin 2θ, U αβ (θ) γ β (U αβ (θ)) -1 = -γ α sin 2θ + γ β cos 2θ. (9
div.p: We show that non-Clifford gates are constructed based on them.
The two-body operation is realized by the unitary dynamics,
div.formula: B αβ (θ) = exp [θγ β γ α ] = exp [iHt/ℏ] ,(10)
div.p: with
div.formula: H = ℏθ t γ β γ α .(11)
div.p: The four-body operation
is introduced [13] as an essential ingredient of universal quantum computation. We generalize it to the four-body operation B
αβγδ (θ) defined by
div.formula: B (4) αβγδ (θ) ≡ exp [iθγ δ γ γ γ β γ α ] = cos θ + iγ δ γ γ γ β γ α sin θ,(13)
div.p: which keeps the parity
div.formula: B (4) αβγδ (θ) , P αβ = 0.(14)
div.p: We use an abbreviation,
The relation
holds because of the coefficient i in Eq.( 13).
It is realized by the unitary dynamics, B
div.formula: αβγδ (θ) = exp [iHt/ℏ] ,(4)
div.p: with
div.formula: H = iℏθ t γ δ γ γ γ β γ α .(18)
div.p: Similary, we may define the 2N -body operation by
div.formula: B (2N ) 1 (θ) ≡ B (2N ) 1∼2N (θ) ≡ exp i (N -1)θ γ 2N γ 2N -1 • • • γ 2 γ 1 = cos θ + i N -1 γ 2N γ 2N -1 • • • γ 2 γ 1 sin θ. (19
div.p: It satisfies the unitary condition,
We also define
It is realized by the 2N -body interaction of Majorana fermions
div.formula: H = i N -1 ℏθ t γ 2N γ 2N -1 • • • γ 2 γ 1 .(22)
div.p: N physical qubits:
We consider the 2N Majorana fermion system. The explicit actions on 2N physical qubits are given by
for odd numbers and
for even numbers, where we have defined the rotation along the z axis by
div.formula: R z (θ) ≡ exp -i θ 2 σ z = diag. e -iθ/2 , e iθ/2 ,(25)
div.p: and
div.formula: U xx (θ) ≡ exp [-iθσ x ⊗ σ x ] .(26)
div.p: N -1 logical qubits: N -1 logical qubits are constructed from N physical qubits based on the correspondence (6).
Local z rotation is possible for any qubits as in
div.formula: B 1 (θ) = exp   -iθ N -1 j=1 σ z   , B 3 (θ) = I 2N -4 ⊗ R z (2θ) = exp [-iθI 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ σ z ] , • • • B 2n-1 (θ) = I 2N -2n ⊗ R z (2θ) ⊗ I 2n-4 = exp [-iθI 2N -2n ⊗ σ z ⊗ I 2n-4 ] , • • • B 2N -3 (θ) = I 2 ⊗ R z (2θ) ⊗ I 2N -2n-6 = exp [-iθI 2 ⊗ σ z ⊗ I 2N -2n-6 ] . (27
div.p: Local x rotation is possible for any qubits as in
where we have defined the rotation along the x axis by
div.formula: R x (θ) ≡ exp -i θ 2 σ x = cos θ 2 -i sin θ 2 -i sin θ 2 cos θ 2 ,(29)
div.p: and
Accordingly, the Hadamard gate is embedded in arbitrary N qubits by using the decomposition formula,
div.formula: U H = R z π 4 R x π 4 R z π 4 .(30)
div.p: Next, we show that it is possible to construct any 2 N diagonal operators based on many-body Majorana interactions, and hence, an arbitrary C n -phase-shift gate is constructed. By applying the Hadamard gate, it is possible to construct the C n NOT gate. There are N C M patterns of 2M -body unitary evolutions in 2N physical qubits. By taking a sum, we have N M =1N C M = 2 N independent physical qubits. They produce 2 N -1 independent logical qubits because there are complementary operators B α (θ) and B α (θ) which produce the same logical qubits. The complementary operators are denoted as

where α indicates a set of indices which is the complementary set of α in the indices 1 ∼ 2N . For example, we consider the case for four qubits N = 4, where eight Majorana fermions exists. The following different braiding operators B 56 and B 123478 give an identical logical quantum gate
where α = 56 and α = 123478 with N = 4. See the full list of the complementary braiding operators for four physical qubits in Eq.(S143) in Supplementary Material.

There are 2 N -1 independent components in N -1 logical qubits. On the other hand, there are 2 N -1 independent manybody Majorana operators. Hence, it is possible to construct arbitrary diagonal operators by solving the linear equation. They include the C n -phase shift gates. Using the relation
div.formula: U C n ϕ = e iϕ 2 n+1 2 n+1 -1 q=1 exp (-1) Mod2 n+1 p=1 pn 2 n+1 n+1 p=1 (σ z ) qp(33
div.p: ) with q p = 0, 1, the C n -phase shift gate is constructed as
div.formula: U C n ϕ = e iϕ 2 n+1 2 n q=1 B odd,q ϕ 2 n+1 2 n -1 r=1 B even,r - ϕ 2 n+1 ,(34)
div.p: where B odd contains odd number of σ z operators for logical qubits, while B even contains even number of σ z operators for logical qubits. By setting ϕ = π, we obtain the C n Z gate.
For example, the CZ gate in three qubits are embedded as

where U p→q CZ indicates that the controlloed qubit is p and the target qubit is q. The CCϕ phase-shift gate acting on three logical qubits in given by Especially, the CCZ gate is constructed as follows,
div.formula: U CCZ = e iπ/8 B 12 π 8 B 34 π 8 B 56 π 8 B 78 π8
div.p: The Toffoli gate is constructed by applying the Hadamard gate to the CCZ gate as in
div.formula: U Toffloi = (I 4 ⊗ U H ) U CCZ (I 4 ⊗ U H ) .(38)
div.p: See Fig. 1(a1). The Fredkin gate is constructed by sequential applications of three Toffoli gates as in
div.formula: U Fredkin = U (3,2)→1 Toffoli U (3,1)→2 Toffoli U (3,2)→1 Toffoli ,(39) where U
div.p: (p,q)→r CZ indicates that the controlloed qubits are p and q while the target qubit is r. See Fig. 1

div.head: (b1).
div.p: The C n NOT gate is constructed from C n Z gate as
div.formula: U C n NOT = (I 2n-2 ⊗ U H ) U C n Z (I 2n-2 ⊗ U H ) ,(40)
div.p: where the Hadamard gate is applied to nth qubit. See Fig. 1(a2). The C n SWAP gate is constructed from the C n Z gate as
div.formula: U C n SWAP = U 1→1 C n NOT U 2→2 C n NOT U 1→1 C n NOT ,(41)
div.p: where U p→p C n NOT indicates that the target qubit is p and the others are controlled qubits, where p indicates the complementary qubits of the qubit p. See Fig. 1

div.head: (b2).
div.p: As a result, an arbitrary phase-shift gate, the C n NOT gate and the Hadamard gate are constructed in any logical qubits, and hence, the universal quantum computation is possible based on many-body interactions.

div.head: Discussions:
div.p: We have analyzed the embedding problem inherent to the Majorana system, and shown that universal quantum computation is possible by introducing many-body interactions of Majorana fermions. Especially, the C n -phase shift gate, the C n NOT and the C n SWAP gate are systematically constructed, which are the basic ingredients of universal quantum computation based on the Solovay-Kitaev theorem. Although it was previously pointed out that four-body interactions of Majorana fermions are enough for universal quantum computation [13], we have shown that the four-body interactions are not enough but 2N -body interactions are necessary.

The proposed quantum gates based on many-body interactions of Majorana fermions are not topologically protected because they are not generated by the standard braiding operations. By combining topological quantum computation based on braiding of Majorana fermions and nontopological quantum computation based on many-body interaction of Majorana fermions, topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum computation is possible. It would be more robust than conventional universal quantum computation because many of quantum gates generated by braiding are topologically protected.
Recently, quantum simulation on Majorana fermions is studied in superconducting qubits [48][49][50]. It will be possible to realize many-body interactions of Majorana fermions in near future.

div.head: Supplemental Material Systematic construction of topological-nontopological hybrid universal quantum gates based on many-body Majorana fermion interactions
div.p: Motohiko Ezawa Department of Applied Physics, The University of Tokyo, 7-3-1 Hongo, Tokyo 113-8656, Japan
div.formula: I. RESULTS ON CONVENTIONAL BRAIDING S1. Embedding
div.p: We consider a one-dimensional chain of Majorana fermions and only consider the braiding between adjacent Majorana fermions. We denote B α ≡ B α,α+1 . The braid operators B α satisfies the Artin braid group relation [1]
div.formula: B α B β = B β B α for |α -β| ≥ 2, B α B α+1 B α = B α+1 B α B α+1 . (S1)
div.p: The embedding of an M -qubit quantum gate to an N -qubit system with M < N is a nontrivial problem in braiding of Majorana fermions. There are two partial solutions. One is setting additional qubits to be 0 as ancilla qubits, where every quantum gates can be embedded. The other is not to use the braiding B 1 . We discuss both of these in what follows.
1. Ancilla embedding N -1 logical qubits are embedded in N logical qubits if the additional qubit is 0,
div.formula: |0n N -1 • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ logical . (S2)
div.p: This is because the correspondence between the physical and logical qubits are identical if the N -th qubit is 0. It is assured by the fact that we can use the same even parity basis in (N -1) qubits because the N th qubit is 0. On the other hand, the action is different if the additional qubit is 1,
div.formula: |1n N -1 • • • n 2 n 1 ⟩ logical . (S3)
div.p: This is because it is necessary to use the odd parity basis in physical N -1 qubits so that total parity is even in the presence of the N th qubit. It is still useful because there are many quantum algorithms where ancilla qubits are 0.

div.head: Braid construction
div.p: We study what M -qubit quantum gates can be embedded to an N -qubit quantum gate with M < N . First, we examine the case for one logical qubit as a simplest example. The braiding B 1 acts differently on the even and odd bases,
div.formula: B even 1 ̸ = B odd 1 ,(S4)
div.p: where
div.formula: B even 1 = e -iπ/4 1 0 0 i , B odd 1 = e -iπ/4 i 0 0 1 . (S5)
div.p: On the other hand, B 2 and B 3 act on even and odd bases in the same way,
div.formula: B even 2 = B odd 2 = 1 √ 2 1 -i -i 1 , B even 3 = B odd 3 = e -iπ/4 1 0 0 i . (S6)
div.p: Similarly, B k for k ≥ 4 has the same action on the even and odd bases. We find that embedding is possible if we do not use the braiding B 1 . Hence, all of the Pauli gates, the Hadamard transformation, the iSWAP gate can be embedded to the N -qubit quantum gates.
On the other hand, the quantum gates which use braiding B 1 cannot be embedded to larger qubit as it is. For example, the CZ gate is given by the braiding e -iπ/4 B -1
div.formula: 5 (B 3 ) -1 B 1 , whose matrix representation is diag. (1, 1, 1, -1, i, -i, -i, -i) , (S7
div.p: once it is embedded to three logical qubits. They are different,
In general, M -quantum gates cannot be embedded in N qubit. We solve the problem by introducing many-body interaction of Majorana fermions in Eq.( 35).

div.head: S2. Single logical qubit
div.p: We discuss how to construct single logical qubit [2]. Two ordinary fermions c 1 and c 2 are introduced from four Majorana fermions as
The basis of physical qubits is given by
div.formula: Ψ physical = |0⟩ , c † 1 |0⟩ , c † 2 |0⟩ , c † 1 c † 2 |0⟩ t ≡ |0, 0⟩ physical , |0, 1⟩ physical , |1, 0⟩ physical , |1, 1⟩ physical t .(S10)
div.p: By taking the even parity basis as
div.formula: |0⟩ |1⟩ logical = |0, 0⟩ |1, 1⟩ physical , (S11
div.p: single logical qubit is constructed from two physical qubits.

div.head: S3. Quantum gates for one logical qubit
div.p: The braid operator B 1 is written in terms of fermion operators,
which operates on two physical qubits (S10) as[2]
Taking the even parity basis, the action is
div.formula: B 1 Ψ logical = e -iπ/4 1 0 0 i |0⟩ |1⟩ logical , (S14
div.p: where the basis for the logical qubit is defined by The braid operation is written as
div.formula: Ψ logical ≡ |0⟩ , c † 1 c † 2 |0⟩ t . (S15) Z 4 X S X g 1 g 2 g 3 g 4 Y H n 1 (a) (b) (c) (d)(e) (f)
B 1 = e -iπ/4 U S ,(S16)
div.p: in terms of the S gate defined by
div.formula: U S ≡ diag. (1, i) . (S17
div.p: The braid operator B 2 is written in terms of fermion operators,
It operates on two physical qubits (S10) as [2],
where
In the even parity basis, the action is
div.formula: B 2 = 1 √ 2 1 -i -i 1 = exp -i π 4 σ x ≡ R x . (S21)
div.p: It has the relation
where U √ X is the square-root of X gate defined by
The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(a). The braiding operator B 3 is written in terms of fermion operators
which operates on two physical qubits (S10) as[2]
In the even parity basis, the action is the same as (S16),
div.formula: B 3 = e -iπ/4 U S ,(S26)
div.p: where the S gate is defined by (S17). The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(b). The Pauli Z gate is given by double braiding of B 3 ,
The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(c). The Pauli X gate (NOT gate) is given [3] by double braiding of B 2 ,
The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(d).
Then, the Pauli Y gate is given by sequential applications of B 2 and B 3 ,
div.formula: U Y ≡ 0 -i i 0 = iU X U Z = -B 2 2 B 2 3 . (S29)
div.p: The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(e). The Hadamard gate is defined by
It is known to be generated by triple braids as [4,5]
div.formula: U H = iB 2 B 3 B 2 . (S31)
div.p: The corresponding braiding is shown in Fig. S1(f).

div.head: S4. One Logical qubit entangled states
div.p: The even cat state is made by applying the Hadamard gate (S30) as
div.formula: U H |0⟩ logical = iB 2 B 3 B 2 |0⟩ logical = 1 √ 2 |0⟩ logical + |1⟩ logical . (S32)
div.p: However, a double braiding is enough for the construction of the even cat state |ψ⟩ even-cat ,
div.formula: e iπ/4 1 B 1 B 2 |0⟩ logical = 1 √ 2 |0⟩ logical + |1⟩ logical ≡ |ψ⟩ even-cat . (S33)
div.p: On the other hand, the odd cat state |ψ⟩ odd-cat is made as
div.formula: e -iπ/4 B -1 1 B 2 |0⟩ logical = 1 √ 2 |0⟩ logical -|1⟩ logical ≡ |ψ⟩ odd-cat .(
Z Z Z X H H g 1 g 2 g 3 g 4 g 5 g 6 XX n 2 n 1 (a) (b) (c) (d) (e)(f) (g)

div.head: S5. Two logical qubits
div.p: In order to construct two logical qubits, we use six Majorana fermions γ 1 , γ 2 , γ 3 , γ 4 , γ 5 and γ 6 . Three ordinary fermion operators are given by
The basis of phycial qubits are given by
div.formula: Ψ physical = (|0⟩ , c † 1 |0⟩ , c † 2 |0⟩ , c † 1 c † 2 |0⟩ , c † 3 |0⟩ , c † 1 c † 3 |0⟩ , c † 2 c † 3 |0⟩ , c † 1 c † 2 c † 3 |0⟩) t ≡ (|0, 0, 0⟩ physical , |0, 0, 1⟩ physical , |0, 1, 0⟩ physical , |0, 1, 1⟩ physical , |1, 0, 0⟩ physical , |1, 0, 1⟩ physical , |1, 1, 0⟩ physical , |1, 1, 1⟩ physical ) t . (S36)
div.p: The explicit braid operators on the physical qubits are
div.formula: B 1 = I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ U S , B 2 = I 2 ⊗ U xx , B 3 = I 2 ⊗ U S ⊗ I 2 , B 4 = U xx ⊗ I 2 , B 5 = U S ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 . (S37)
div.p: Two logical qubits are constructed from three physical qubits as
In the logical qubit basis, the braiding operators are
div.formula: B 1 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, i, i, 1) , B 2 = I 2 ⊗ R x , B 3 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, i, 1, i) , B 4 = U xx , B 5 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, 1, i, i) . (S39
div.p: where R x is defined by (S21) and U xx is defined by (S20).

div.head: S6. Pauli gates
div.p: The two-qubit Pauli gates are defined by
where k 1 and k 2 take 0, x, y and z. The Pauli Z gates are generated by braiding B 2k+1 with odd indices,
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ σ Z = iB 2 3 , σ Z ⊗ I 2 = iB 2 5 , σ Z ⊗ σ Z = -B 2 5 B 2 3 . (S41)
div.p: They are summarized as
div.formula: (σ Z ) n2 ⊗ (σ Z ) n1 = iB 2 5 n2 iB 2 3 n1 , (S42
div.p: where n 1 and n 2 take 0 or 1.
The Pauli X gates are generated by braiding with even indices B 2k ,
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ σ X = iB 2 2 , σ X ⊗ σ X = iB 2 4 , I 2 ⊗ σ X = -B 2 4 B 2 2 . (S43
div.p: It should be noted that B 2 4 does not generate I 2 ⊗ σ X but generate σ X ⊗ σ X . We show the braiding for Pauli gates in Fig. S2. Pauli Y gates are generated by sequential applications of Pauli X gates and Pauli Z gates based on the relation U Y = iU X U Z . Thus, all of Pauli gates for two qubits can be generated by braiding.

div.head: S7. Hadamard gates
div.p: The Hadamard gate acting on the first qubit can be embedded as
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ U H = iB 2 B 3 B 2 .
div.p: The Hadamard gate acting on the second qubit can be embedded as
div.formula: U H ⊗ I 2 = -B 1 B 2 B 3 B 4 B 3 B 2 B 1 . (S45)
div.p: It requires more braiding than the previous results [5,6], where three braiding are enough. It is due to the choice of the correspondence between the physical and logical qubits.

div.head: S8. Quantum gates for two logical qubits
div.p: It is known that the controlled-Z (CZ) gate
is generated as [6] U CZ = e -iπ/4 B -1
See Fig. S3(a).
It is also known that the controlled-NOT (CNOT) gate
is generated by 7 braiding [4,6,7], wher braiding are given by
div.formula: U CNOT = -e -iπ/4 B -1 5 B 1 B 2 B 3 B 1 B 2 B 1 . (S49)
div.p: See Fig. S3(b). On the other hand, there is a quantum circuit decomposition formula which involves 9 braiding. The SWAP gate is defined by
div.formula: U CNOT = (I 2 ⊗ U H ) U CZ (I 2 ⊗ U H ) ,(
div.p: which is realized by 7 braiding as
See Fig. S3(c). This is smaller than the previous result using 15 braiding [6] based on the quantum circuit decomposition
div.formula: U SWAP = (I 2 ⊗ U H ) U CZ (I 2 ⊗ U H ) (U H ⊗ I 2 ) U CZ (U H ⊗ I 2 ) (I 2 ⊗ U H ) U CZ (I 2 ⊗ U H ) . (S53)
div.p: We list up various quantum gates generated by braiding.
The anti-CNOT gate is defined by [8]
which is generated by 7 braiding
div.formula: U CX = e iπ/4 B -1 5 B -1 1 B -1 2 B 3 B 1 B 2 B 1 . (S55)
div.p: It can be decomposed into U CX = (I 2 ⊗ U X ) U CNOT . If we use this relation, 9 braiding are necessary. See Fig. S3(d).
The iSWAP gate is defined by
which is realized by the six braiding
div.formula: U iSWAP = -B 3 B 4 B 5 B 3 B 4 B 3 . (S57) See Fig.S3(e).
div.p: The double CNOT gate is defined by[9]
which is realized by
div.formula: U DCNOT = B -1 2 B -1 3 B -1 4 B -1 5 B 1 B 2 B 3 B 4 .(S59)
div.p: See Fig. S3(f). The Mølmer-Sørensen gate is defined by [10]
which is realized by
div.formula: U MS = -iB 3 B 4 B 5 B 4 B 3 B 1 B 1 .(S61)
div.p: See Fig. S3(g). The cross-resonance gate is defined by [11]
which is realized by
div.formula: U CR = -B 4 B 4 B 1 B 2 B 3 B 2 B 1 .(S63)
div.p: See Fig. S3(h).
We define the entangled Hadamard gate by
which is realized by
div.formula: U (2) H = -e -iπ/4 B 5 B 4 B 3 B 2 B 1 .(
div.p: We note that it is obtained by a permutation of the third and fourth columns of the cross product of the Hadamard gates given by
which leads to a relation
div.formula: U H ⊗ U H = U CNOT U(2)
div.p: H .

div.head: (S68)
div.p: Hence, it is realized by
div.formula: U H ⊗ U H = -B -1 5 B 1 B 2 B 3 B 1 B 2 B 1 B 5 B 4 B 3 B 2 B 1 .(S69) Both U (2)
div.p: H and U H ⊗ U H are the Hadamard gates and they are useful for various quantum algorithms.

div.head: S9. Equal-coefficient states
div.p: The equal-coefficient state is constructed as
div.formula: iB 1 B 2 B 3 B 4 B 5 |0, 0⟩ logical = 1 2 |0, 0⟩ logical + |0, 1⟩ logical + |1, 0⟩ logical + |1, 1⟩ logical ≡ 1 2 |0⟩ decimal logical + |1⟩ decimal logical + |2⟩ decimal logical + |3⟩ decimal logical ,(S70)
div.p: where |j⟩ decimal logical is a decimal representation of qubits. It is a fundamental entangled state for two qubits.

div.head: S10. Three logical qubits
div.p: We use eight Majorana fermions in order to construct three logical qubits,
The explicit braid actions on the physical qubits are
div.formula: B 1 = I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ U S , B 2 = I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ U xx , B 3 = I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ U S ⊗ I 2 , B 4 = I 2 ⊗ U xx ⊗ I 2 , B 5 = I 2 ⊗ U S ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 , B 6 = U xx ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 , B 7 = U S ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 . (S72)
div.p: Three logical qubits are constructed from four physical qubits as
Explicit matrix representations for the braiding operator are
div.formula: B 1 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, i, i, 1, i, 1, 1, i) = exp - iπ 4 σ z ⊗ σ z ⊗ σ z , B 2 = I 4 ⊗ R x , B 3 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, i, 1, i, 1, i, 1, i) = e -iπ/4 I 4 ⊗ U S , B 4 = I 2 ⊗ U xx , B 5 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, 1, i, i, 1, 1, i, i) = e -iπ/4 I 2 ⊗ U S ⊗ I 2 , B 6 = U xx ⊗ I 2 , B 7 = e -iπ/4 diag. (1, 1, 1, 1, i, i, i, i) = e -iπ/4 U S ⊗ I 4 .(S74)

div.head: S11. Pauli gates
div.p: The three-qubit Pauli gates are defined by
div.formula: σ k3 ⊗ σ k2 ⊗ σ k1 ,(S75)
div.p: where k 1 , k 2 and k 3 take 0, x, y and z. The Pauli Z gates are generated by braiding operators B 2k+1 with odd indices
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ σ Z = iB 2 3 , I 2 ⊗ σ Z ⊗ I 2 = iB 2 5 , σ Z ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 = iB 2 7 ,(S76)
div.p: They are summarized as
div.formula: (σ Z ) n3 ⊗ (σ Z ) n2 ⊗ (σ Z ) n1 = iB 2 7 n3 iB 2 5 n2 iB 2 3 n1 ,(S77)
div.p: where n 1 n 2 and n 3 take 0 or 1.
The Pauli X gates are generated by braiding operators with even numbers,
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ σ X = iB 2 2 , I 2 ⊗ σ X ⊗ σ X = iB 2 4 , σ X ⊗ σ X ⊗ I 2 = iB 2 6 . (S78)
div.p: We show the corresponding braiding in Fig. S4. It is impossible to construct logical gates corresponding to
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ σ X ⊗ I 2 and σ X ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 (S79)
div.p: solely by braiding. This problem is solved by introducing many-body interactions of Majorana fermions as in Eq.(S142).
The other Pauli gates can be generated by sequential applications of the above Pauli gates.

div.head: S12. Diagonal braiding
div.p: We first search braiding operators for the quantum gates generated by odd double braiding, There are eight patterns represented by the Pauli Z gates
div.formula: U diag = B 2 7 n3 B 2 5 n2 B 2 3 n1 . (S80) Z Z X g 1 g 2 g 3 g 4 g 5 g 6 g 7 g 8 Z n 2 n 3 n 1 (a) (b) (c) (d)(e) (f)
div.p: Next, we search real and diagonal gates obtained by the following odd braiding
We search states whose components are ±1. There are four additional quantum gates, whose traces are zero TrU diag = 0,
In addition, there are additional quantum gates, whose traces are nonzero TrU diag ̸ = 0,

It is natural to anticipate that the CZ gate and the CCZ gate are generated by even braiding because they are diagonal gates. However, this is not the case by checking all 4 3 patterns of braiding. As a result, the even braiding do not generate the CZ gates
and the CCZ gate
This problem is solved by introducing many-body interactions of Majorana fermions as shown in the main text.

div.head: S13. Hadamard gates
div.p: The Hadamard gate can be embedded in the first qubit as
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ I 2 ⊗ U H = iB 2 B 3 B 2 ,(S87)
div.p: as in the case of (S44). We also find that the Hadamard gate can be embedded in the third qubit as
div.formula: U H ⊗ I 2 ⊗ I 2 = -iB 1 B 2 B 3 B 4 B 5 B 6 B 5 B 4 B 3 B 2 B 1 . (S88)
div.p: On the other hand, it is very hard to embed the Hadamard gate in the second qubit
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ U H ⊗ I 2 .
div.p: It is possible by introducing many-body interactions of Majorana fermions. The Hadamard gate for the N -th qubit is given by
S14. Two-qubit quantum gates embedded in three-qubit quantum gates
The iSWAP gate can be embedded to a three-qubit topological gate because it does not involve B 1 and is given by
div.formula: I 2 ⊗ U iSWAP = -B 3 B 4 B 5 B 3 B 4 B 3 .(S90)
div.p: See Fig. S5(a). We also find the iSWAP gate can be embedded as
div.formula: U iSWAP ⊗ I 2 = -B 5 B 6 B 7 B 5 B 6 B 5 .(

div.head: S15. Three-qubit quantum gates
div.p: We find that the three-qubit Hadamard transformation is generated as There is a relation
div.formula: U H ⊗ U H ⊗ U H = -U 3P U (3) H ,(S94)
div.p: where U 3P is defined by
It is impossible to generate the W state by braiding
div.formula: |W⟩ logical = 1 √ 3 |000⟩ logical + |010⟩ logical + |100⟩ logical ,(S96)
div.p: because the number of the nonzero terms of the W state is 3, which contradicts the fact that the number of the nonzero terms must be 1, 2, 4 and 8 for three qubit states generated by braiding.

div.head: S16. N logical qubits
div.p: The braid representation of 2N + 2 Majorana fermions is equivalent to the π/2 rotation in SO(2N + 2), suggested by the fact that braid operators are represented by the Gamma matrices [12,13]. The number of the braid group is given by [14] |Image
div.formula: (B 2n )| = 2 2n-1 (2n)! for n=even 2 2n (2n)! for n=odd . (S97)
div.p: The iSWAP gate is embedded as
div.formula: I k-2 2 ⊗ U iSWAP ⊗ I N -k 2 ∝ B 2k+1 B 2k+2 B 2k+3 B 2k+1 B 2k+2 B 2k+1 . (S98)

div.head: Diagonal braiding
div.p: We consider odd braiding defined by

where n k is an integer satisfying 1 ≤ n k ≤ N + 1. They are Abelian braiding because there are no adjacent braiding. Then, there are only 4 k patterns. Especially, we consider odd double braiding defined by
are interesting because they are identical to
div.formula: (B odd ) 2 = (σ Z ) m k (σ Z ) m2 • • • (σ Z ) m1 ,(S101)
div.p: where m k = 0, 1. Namely, every Pauli gates constructing from the Pauli Z gate can be generated. Next, we consider even braiding defined by
div.formula: B even (n 1 , n 2 , • • • , n k ) ≡ B 2n k B 2n k-1 • • • B 2n1 . (S102)
div.p: They are also the Abelian braiding, where each braiding commutes each other. We also consider even double braiding defined by
div.formula: (B even ) 2 ≡ iB 2 2n k iB 2 2n k-1 • • • iB 2 2n1 . (S103)
div.p: On the other hand, it is impossible to construct the Pauli X gate except for the first qubit. See Eq.( 24) in the main text.

div.head: Hadamard transformation
div.p: The Hadamard transformation is used for the initial process of various quantum algorithm such as the Kitaev phase estimation algorithm, the Deutsch algorithm, the Deutsch-Jozsa algorithm, the Simon algorithm, the Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm, the Grover algorithm and the Shor algorithm. It is generated by the braiding
div.formula: U (N ) H ∝ B 2N +1 B 2N • • • B 2 B 1 .
div.p: The equal-coefficient state is generated as
div.formula: U (N ) H |0, 0⟩ logical ∝ 2 N j=1 |j⟩ logical ,(S105)
div.p: where |j⟩ logical is the decimal representation of the qubit. In the even parity basis, the action is
div.formula: B 2 (θ) = cos θ -i sin θ -i sin θ cos θ ≡ R x (2θ) ,(S114)
div.p: which is identical to the rotation along the x axis
div.formula: R y (θ) = R z π 2 R x (θ) R z - π 2 . (S121)

figure.head: FIG. 1 .
figure.figDesc: FIG. 1. (a1) Construction of the CCNOT gate from the CCZ gate and the Hadamard gates. (a2) Construction of the C n NOT gate from the C n Z gate and the Hadamard gates. (b1) Construction of the Fredkin gate from three Toffoli gates. (b2) Construction of the C n SWAP gate from the C n NOT gates.

figure.head: FIG
figure.figDesc: FIG. S1. (a) Square-root of NOT gate, (b) S gate, (c) Pauli Z gate, (d) Pauli X gate, (e) Pauli Y gate and (f) Hadamard gate.

figure.head: FIG. S2 .
figure.label: S2
figure.figDesc: FIG. S2. The braiding process for Pauli gates. (a) Pauli Z gate embedded to the first qubit, (b) Pauli Z gate embedded to the second qubit, (c) Two Pauli Z gates are embedded to the first and the second qubits, (d) Pauli X gate embedded to the first qubit, (e) Two Pauli X gates are embedded to the first and the second qubits, (f) Hadamard gate embedded to the first qubit and (g) Hadamard gate embedded to the second qubit.

FIG. S3. Braiding process for various two-qubit quantum gates. (a) CZ gate, (b) CNOT gate, (c) SWAP gate, (d) anti-CX gate, (e) iSWAP gate, (f) DCNOT gate, (g) Molmer-Sorensen gate, (h) cross-resonance gate (i) Hadamard gate.
S65) See Fig.S3(i). It is different from the cross product of the Hadamard gates U

FIG. S4. Pauli gates embedded in three qubits. (a) Pauli Z gate embedded to the first qubit, (b) Pauli Z gate embedded to the second qubit, (c) Pauli Z gate embedded to the third qubit, (d) Pauli X gate embedded to the first qubit, (e) Two Pauli X gates are embedded to the first and second qubits and (f) Two Pauli X gates are embedded to the second third qubits.
See Fig.S5(b). It is different from the cross-product of the Hadamard gate

figure.head: 8 FIG
figure.figDesc: FIG. S5. (a) and (b) iSWAP gate embedded to three qubit systems. (c) Three-qubit Hadamard transformation.

figure.head: B 2
figure.figDesc: II. 2N -BODY UNITARY EVOLUTION S1. Quantum gates for one logical qubitThe 2-body Majorana operator B 1 (θ) is written in terms of fermion operators,B 1 (θ) = cos θ + γ 2 γ 1 sin θ = cos θ + ic † 1 c 1 -ic 1 c † 1 sin θ ,(S106)which operates on two physical qubits (S10) as B 1 (θ) Ψ physical = the even parity basis, the action isB 1 (θ) Ψ logical = e -iθ 1 0 0 e 2iθ |0⟩ |1⟩ logical . (S108)It is the arbitrary phase-shift gate. Especially, by setting θ = π/8, the T gate is constructedU T ≡ diag. 1, e iπ/4 . (S109)It is identical to the rotation along the z axisB 1 (θ) = R z (2θ), is written in terms of fermion operators,B 2 (θ) = cos θ + γ 3 γ 2 sin θ = cos θ + ic 2 c † 1 + ic † 2 c † 1 -ic 2 c 1 -ic † 2 c 1 sin θ,(S112)which operates on two physical qubits (S10) as[2],B 2 (θ) Ψ physical =

figure.head: with R x (θ) ≡ exp -i θ 2 σ x = cos θ 2 -i sin θ 2 -i sin θ 2 cos θ 2 .
figure.figDesc: (S116)The operator B 3 (θ) is written in terms of fermion operatorsB 3 (θ) = cos θ + γ 4 γ 3 sin θ = cos θ + ic † 2 c 2 -ic 2 c † 2 sin θ,(S117)which operates on two physical qubits (S10) as[2] B 3 (θ) Ψ physical = parity basis, the action is the same as (S108), B 3 (θ) = e -iθ 1 the y axis defined byR y (θ) ≡ exp -i θ 2 σ y (S120)is realized by the sequential operations

div.p: This work is supported by CREST, JST (Grants No. JP-MJCR20T2) and Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research from MEXT KAKENHI (Grant No. 23H00171).

org.orgName: Grants-in-Aid for Scientific Research from MEXT KAKENHI

div.head: S2. Three physical qubits
div.p: Next, we study the six Majorana fermion system. The explicit actions on the physical qubits are

div.head: S3. Two logical qubits
div.p: Two logical qubits are constructed from three physical qubits by taking the even parity basis. The action of B 1 (θ) to the logical qubit is B 1 (θ) = diag. e -iθ , e iθ , e iθ , e -iθ , (S123) which is identical to the ZZ interaction
with
The action of B 4 (θ) to the logical qubit is
which is identical to the xx interaction
with
The action of B 3 (θ) and B 5 (θ) to the logical qubit is
The action of B 2 (θ) to the logical qubit is
which is rewritten in the form of

div.head: S4. Controlled phase-shift gate
div.p: We find θ1+θ2+θ3) , e i(θ1+θ2-θ3) , e i(θ1-θ2+θ3) , e i(-θ1+θ2+θ3) . (S132)
The controlled phase-shift gate with arbitrary phase is constructed by setting
Especially, the CZ gate is constructed by setting θ = π/4.

div.head: S5. Controlled-unitary gate
div.p: It is known that the controlled unitary gate is constructed as [22] U
with
because
and
In the Majorana system, the basic rotations are not along the y axis but the x axis. The similar decomposition is possible only by using the rotations along the z and x axes as
The proof is similar. First, we have
where we have used the relation
for j = x, y and z. Next, we have
where we have used the relation
Hence, the controlled unitary gate is implemented by 2-body Majorana interaction.

div.head: S6. Four physical qubits
div.p: We consider eight Majorana fermion system The explicit actions on four physical qubits are given by
We summarize results on constructing full set of Pauli Z gate for three logical qubits in the following table:
We have the identical logical qubits:
S7. Three logical qubits Three logical qubits are constructed from four physical qubits by taking the even parity basis.
B 12 = diag. e -iθ , e iθ , e iθ , e -iθ , e iθ , e -iθ , e -iθ , e

We find that controlled-controlled phase shift gate cannot be implemented only by diagonal braiding. It is proved by counting the number of the degrees of freedom. We need to tune 7 parameters for the diagonal quantum gates. On the other hand, there are only three independent angle because the diagonal operators are B The Toffoli gate is constructed by applying the Hadamard gate to the CCZ gate as in
See Fig. 1(a). The Fredkin gate is constructed by sequential applications of three Toffoli gates as in
See Fig. 1(b). The CZ gate in three qubits are embedded as

div.head: S8. Logical 4 qubits
div.p: We summarize results on constructing full set of Pauli Z gate for four logical qubits in the following table:
where 0 is an abbreviation of 10. The CCCϕ gate is explicitly constructed as We consider the case θ = π/4. The Artin braid group relation reads [1],
It is identical to the extraspecial 2 group[23]
by setting
It is straightforward to show that
α+1 M (4) α , M (4) α M
α+3 M (4) α , M (4) a M
when we set
It is a generalization of the extraspecial 2 group. Correspondingly, we obtain a generalized braiding group relation
and
for |α -β| = 2 and |α -β| ≥ 4. In the similar way, we find

Multiparticle Entanglement of Hot Trapped Ions
